NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING (NGS) COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
PATIENT INFORMATION
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Patient Name: Raymond Kowalski
Patient DOB: [ON FILE]
MRN: [ON FILE]
Ordering Physician: [Hepatopancreatobiliary Oncology Service]
Diagnosis: Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma, Head of Pancreas
Stage at Submission: cT3N1M0, Stage III (AJCC 8th ed.) вЂ” Borderline Resectable

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Specimen Type: Formalin-Fixed, Paraffin-Embedded (FFPE) Cell Block вЂ” EUS-Guided Fine Needle Aspiration (FNA)
Specimen Site: Head of Pancreas (Primary Tumor)
Procedure: Endoscopic Ultrasound-Guided Fine Needle Aspiration (EUS-FNA)
Tumor Cellularity (Pathologist Estimate): 35% (adequate for sequencing; threshold met)
DNA Input Quality: Adequate; sequencing QC metrics within acceptable parameters
Assay Platform: Hybrid-capture-based comprehensive genomic profiling (CGP); 324-gene panel; tumor DNA with matched leukocyte normal DNA for somatic/germline differentiation
Mean Coverage (Tumor): 682x
Mean Coverage (Normal): 314x

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB)
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
TMB: 3.2 mutations/Mb
Interpretation: LOW (Threshold for TMB-High: в‰Ґ10 mutations/Mb per FDA guidance)
Clinical Significance: Patient does NOT qualify for pembrolizumab (Keytruda) based on TMB-High criteria. Low TMB is expected and characteristic of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI) STATUS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
MSI Status: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
Method: Fragment analysis of 95 microsatellite loci across the sequenced genome
MMR Protein IHC (concurrent assessment): MLH1 вЂ” intact nuclear expression; MSH2 вЂ” intact nuclear expression; MSH6 вЂ” intact nuclear expression; PMS2 вЂ” intact nuclear expression
Germline MMR Gene Mutations (Somatic/Germline Analysis): MLH1 вЂ” No pathogenic variant detected; MSH2 вЂ” No pathogenic variant detected; MSH6 вЂ” No pathogenic variant detected; PMS2 вЂ” No pathogenic variant detected
Interpretation: MISMATCH REPAIR PROFICIENT (pMMR). Patient does NOT qualify for checkpoint inhibitor therapy (anti-PD-1/PD-L1) based on MSI/MMR status. Approximately 1-2% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas harbor MSI-H/dMMR; this patient's tumor does not belong to this subset.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION DEFICIENCY (HRD) STATUS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
HRD Score: 12 (Low; threshold for HRD-positive: в‰Ґ42 by validated genomic scar algorithm)
BRCA1: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant detected (Wild-Type)
BRCA2: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant detected (Wild-Type)
ATM: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant detected (Wild-Type)
PALB2: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant detected (Wild-Type)
RAD51C: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant detected
RAD51D: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant detected
BAP1: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant detected
Interpretation: HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION PROFICIENT (HRP / HRD-NEGATIVE). Patient is unlikely to derive exceptional benefit from PARP inhibitor therapy (e.g., olaparib [Lynparza], rucaparib). Platinum-based chemotherapy sensitivity from HRD cannot be anticipated on this basis; however, platinum (oxaliplatin) may still be incorporated into standard regimens.
Note: Per NCCN Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma Guidelines, germline testing for BRCA1/2, ATM, PALB2, and Lynch syndrome genes is recommended for all patients with pancreatic adenocarcinoma regardless of family history. Somatic analysis is reported above; dedicated germline testing with appropriate genetic counseling is strongly recommended and should be obtained independently.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
PD-L1 EXPRESSION
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
PD-L1 IHC (22C3 Pharmx Assay, Dako):
  Combined Positive Score (CPS): 1
  Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): <1%
Interpretation: LOW PD-L1 expression. Does not support benefit from anti-PD-1 monotherapy in this histology. Consistent with the immunologically "cold" tumor microenvironment typical of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
SOMAIC GENOMIC ALTERATIONS вЂ” PATHOGENIC / LIKELY PATHOGENIC
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

1. KRAS вЂ” p.G12V (c.35G>T) вЂ” Missense Mutation
   Chromosome: 12p12.1
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 42%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC вЂ” Oncogenic Driver
   Copy Number: Diploid background
   Clonality: Clonal (high VAF consistent with early trunk driver event)
   Functional Consequence: Constitutive activation of RAS/MAPK signaling pathway; GTPase activity impaired; resistance to intrinsic and GAP-mediated GTP hydrolysis. KRAS p.G12V (Glyв†’Val substitution) results in persistent GTP-bound, active conformation of KRAS protein, driving uncontrolled cell proliferation, survival, and invasion via RAF/MEK/ERK and PI3K/AKT/mTOR effector cascades.
   Prevalence: KRAS mutations are found in approximately 90-95% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas. The G12V substitution represents approximately 20-25% of all KRAS mutations in PDAC (G12D is most common at ~40%).
   Therapeutic Implications: KRAS G12V mutation does NOT confer eligibility for KRAS G12C inhibitors (sotorasib [Lumakras], adagrasib [Krazati]) вЂ” these are cysteine-selective covalent inhibitors and are specifically inactive against the G12V valine substitution. No FDA-approved targeted therapy directly addressing KRAS G12V is currently available. Pan-KRAS inhibitors and KRAS G12V-selective agents remain investigational. Enrollment in KRAS G12V-targeting clinical trials should be considered. Downstream pathway inhibition (MEK inhibitors, ERK inhibitors) has shown limited single-agent activity in PDAC and is not standard of care.
   Evidence Level: Tier I вЂ” FDA-recognized oncogenic driver; no approved matched targeted therapy for this specific allele.

2. TP53 вЂ” p.R248W (c.742C>T) вЂ” Missense Mutation
   Chromosome: 17p13.1
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 38%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC вЂ” Loss-of-Function / Dominant-Negative
   Copy Number: Concurrent allelic loss (LOH at 17p) вЂ” biallelic inactivation presumed
   Functional Consequence: p.R248W is one of the most common and well-characterized TP53 hotspot mutations. The arginine-to-tryptophan substitution at codon 248 disrupts direct DNA contact within the DNA-binding domain, abolishing sequence-specific transcriptional activation of p53 target genes (including p21/CDKN1A, MDM2, PUMA, BAX). Additionally, p.R248W displays dominant-negative activity by co-precipitating with wild-type p53 tetramers, and exhibits gain-of-function oncogenic properties including enhanced cell migration, invasion, and chemoresistance through interactions with p63/p73 family members and chromatin-remodeling complexes.
   Prevalence: TP53 mutations are found in approximately 50-75% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas. The R248W hotspot is among the most frequently encountered.
   Therapeutic Implications: No direct targeted therapy approved for TP53 p.R248W. Strong nuclear p53 IHC staining (gain-of-function mutant pattern) may be used as a surrogate marker in pathologic assessment. Loss of normal p53-mediated DNA damage checkpoint function may theoretically increase sensitivity to DNA-damaging agents (e.g., platinum compounds, irinotecan) вЂ” however, this is a general consideration and not a validated predictive biomarker in PDAC.
   Evidence Level: Tier I вЂ” Well-established oncogenic driver; no approved matched targeted therapy.

3. SMAD4 (DPC4) вЂ” Homozygous Deletion
   Chromosome: 18q21.2
   Copy Number: 0 copies (homozygous loss confirmed; log2 ratio -2.8)
   Classification: PATHOGENIC вЂ” Tumor Suppressor Loss
   Functional Consequence: Complete biallelic loss of SMAD4, the central mediator of canonical TGF-ОІ/BMP signaling. SMAD4 loss disrupts TGF-ОІ-mediated cytostatic responses (cell cycle arrest, apoptosis, senescence) and paradoxically promotes TGF-ОІ-driven pro-tumorigenic effects including epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT), invasion, and immunosuppression. In pancreatic cancer, SMAD4 loss is a late-stage event typically occurring after KRAS activation and CDKN2A/TP53 loss, consistent with classic PDAC progression.
   Prevalence: SMAD4 deletions or inactivating mutations are found in approximately 50-60% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas.
   Prognostic Significance: SMAD4 loss is associated with a greater propensity for systemic (distant) metastatic disease as opposed to predominantly locoregional failure. Some retrospective data suggest SMAD4 loss predicts for worse outcomes with locally ablative/radiotherapy approaches, though this remains investigational. SMAD4 status may inform post-resection patterns of failure.
   Therapeutic Implications: No approved targeted therapy. Investigational: TGF-ОІ pathway inhibitors under study. SMAD4 status may be relevant when considering neoadjuvant systemic approaches vs. radiation consolidation strategies.
   Evidence Level: Tier I вЂ” Well-established tumor suppressor in PDAC; no approved matched targeted therapy.

4. CDKN2A вЂ” Homozygous Deletion
   Chromosome: 9p21.3
   Copy Number: 0 copies (homozygous deletion confirmed; includes both p16/INK4A and p14/ARF reading frames)
   Classification: PATHOGENIC вЂ” Tumor Suppressor Loss
   Functional Consequence: CDKN2A encodes two critical tumor suppressor proteins via alternative reading frames: p16/INK4A (CDK4/6 inhibitor вЂ” prevents Rb phosphorylation and G1/S cell cycle progression) and p14/ARF (MDM2 inhibitor вЂ” stabilizes p53). Homozygous deletion of CDKN2A eliminates both tumor-suppressive functions. Combined loss of p16 (CDKN2A) and TP53 function represents two of the four canonical genetic "hits" in the Vogelstein model of PDAC progression (alongside KRAS activation and SMAD4 loss).
   Prevalence: CDKN2A loss (deletion or promoter hypermethylation) is found in approximately 95% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas вЂ” one of the highest frequencies of any cancer type.
   Therapeutic Implications: Theoretically, CDKN2A loss and consequent CDK4/6 dependency might suggest sensitivity to CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib [Ibrance], ribociclib [Kisqali], abemaciclib [Verzenio]). However, KRAS-mutant tumors typically maintain bypass mechanisms through cyclin D1 upregulation and other RB pathway perturbations, and CDK4/6 inhibitors have not demonstrated substantial single-agent activity in KRAS-mutant PDAC in early-phase trials. Combinatorial strategies remain under investigation.
   Evidence Level: Tier II вЂ” Established PDAC driver; CDK4/6 inhibitor data insufficient for standard use.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS (OTHER SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS)
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

MYC вЂ” Low-Level Amplification
   Chromosome: 8q24.21
   Copy Number: Estimated 6-8 copies (log2 ratio +1.2)
   Classification: VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE вЂ” Potentially Relevant
   Functional Consequence: MYC is a transcription factor central to cell proliferation, ribosome biogenesis, and metabolic reprogramming. Low-level MYC amplification (as opposed to high-level amplification) is a common finding in PDAC and may contribute to increased proliferative signaling. Direct MYC inhibition remains a major unmet therapeutic goal; no approved direct MYC inhibitor is currently available.
   Therapeutic Implications: Investigational. BET bromodomain inhibitors, CDK7/9 inhibitors, and KRAS-synthetic lethal approaches that indirectly reduce MYC transcription are under study.

ERBB2 (HER2) вЂ” No Amplification
   Copy Number: 2.8 (borderline; not amplified)
   Classification: NEGATIVE вЂ” Not Clinically Actionable at this threshold
   Note: HER2 amplification is found in approximately 2% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas. This patient's ERBB2 copy number does not meet criteria for HER2 amplification (threshold typically в‰Ґ6 copies or ratio в‰Ґ2.0 relative to CEP17). Anti-HER2 therapy (trastuzumab [Herceptin], pertuzumab, trastuzumab deruxtecan [Enhertu]) is not indicated on this basis.

GENE FUSIONS AND REARRANGEMENTS

GERMLINE VARIANT ASSESSMENT (SOMATIC/GERMLINE DIFFERENTIATION)
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
All variants identified above are consistent with somatic origin based on matched normal (leukocyte) analysis. No variants consistent with germline origin were identified in the cancer predisposition genes assessed on this panel.

Germline Genes with No Somatic Pathogenic Variants Detected (Relevant to Hereditary Pancreatic Cancer):
  BRCA1: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant
  BRCA2: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant
  ATM: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant
  PALB2: No pathogenic somatic or germline variant
  CDKN2A (germline): No pathogenic germline variant detected in the CDKN2A gene (somatic deletion noted above)
  STK11: No pathogenic germline variant
  MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2: No pathogenic germline variants (Lynch syndrome excluded)

IMPORTANT NOTICE: This report does NOT substitute for formal clinical germline genetic testing. Per NCCN Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma Guidelines (v.2024), all patients with pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma should be referred for dedicated germline genetic counseling and testing regardless of family history or somatic findings. Germline BRCA1/2, ATM, PALB2, and Lynch syndrome testing should be pursued through a CLIA-certified clinical germline laboratory with appropriate pre- and post-test genetic counseling.

CLINICAL TRIAL MATCHING SUMMARY
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Based on the genomic profile identified:

  KRAS G12V Targeting:
    - Investigational KRAS G12V-selective inhibitors (emerging; distinct from G12C-specific agents)
    - Pan-RAS / pan-KRAS inhibitors (e.g., RMC-6236, MRTX1133 in combination studies)
    - KRAS G12V x CD3 bispecific T-cell engager approaches (early-phase)
    - MEK/ERK downstream inhibitor combinations

  CDKN2A-Loss Context:
    - CDK4/6 inhibitor combinations in KRAS-mutant pancreatic cancer
    - CDK6-specific strategies

  MSS / pMMR / TMB-Low Context:
    - Novel immunotherapy combinations designed to overcome immune exclusion in pMMR PDAC
    - CAR-T cell and mRNA vaccine approaches targeting KRAS mutant neoepitopes (KRAS G12V is an immunogenic neoantigen of particular interest)

  SMAD4-Loss Context:
    - TGF-ОІ pathway inhibitor trials
    - Studies stratifying by SMAD4 status for local vs. systemic therapy approaches

  Clinical trial matching is recommended via ClinicalTrials.gov or institutional tumor board review.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
MOLECULAR SUMMARY AND INTERPRETATION
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
This specimen harbors the four canonical somatic alterations that define the classic progression model of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma:

  1. KRAS p.G12V (VAF 42%) вЂ” Clonal oncogenic driver; constitutive RAS/MAPK activation
  2. CDKN2A homozygous deletion вЂ” Cell cycle deregulation; loss of p16/INK4A and p14/ARF
  3. TP53 p.R248W (VAF 38%) вЂ” DNA damage checkpoint abrogation; dominant-negative + gain-of-function
  4. SMAD4 homozygous deletion вЂ” TGF-ОІ pathway loss; associated with systemic metastatic phenotype

This molecular profile ("KCST" quad-mutation) is found in approximately 10-15% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas and represents one of the most genomically altered subtypes. It is associated with an aggressive biological phenotype and highlights the importance of effective systemic therapy.

NO actionable targeted therapy is currently available for this genomic profile based on currently approved drugs and validated biomarker-drug pairings. The KRAS mutation is NOT the G12C variant; sotorasib and adagrasib do not apply. The tumor is MSS and TMB-low, precluding pembrolizumab on these grounds. There is no HRD or BRCA/ATM deficiency to support PARP inhibitor use. There are no NTRK, ALK, RET, or ROS1 fusions.

Standard-of-care cytotoxic chemotherapy remains the backbone of therapy. FOLFIRINOX (oxaliplatin, irinotecan, leucovorin, 5-fluorouracil) and gemcitabine + nab-paclitaxel (Abraxane) are the preferred first-line regimens in PDAC with adequate performance status (ECOG 0-1). The SMAD4 deletion in this patient warrants attention in multidisciplinary discussion of local versus systemic therapy strategy.

Germline genetic testing is strongly recommended per NCCN guidelines.
